popup.title: Threat Magnifier 2.0
popup.advancedDesc: Shows detailed threat analysis and website previews. If disabled, only a color flag is shown.
popup.blockDangerDesc: Prevents clicking links classified as dangerous. A warning will appear with an option to proceed anyway.
popup.apiDesc: Used to score external links in Advanced Mode.

content.blockedDesc: This link was classified as dangerous. Click "Continue Anyway" if you trust it.
content.vtKeyMissing: VirusTotal API key missing. Add it in options to score this link.
content.safeNoThreat: Looks safe. No obvious structural threats detected.
content.vtNoData: VirusTotal: No scan data available for this specific URL.
content.vtInvalidKey: VirusTotal: Invalid API Key or Quota Exceeded.
content.reasonLinkInlineJsShort: Contains inline JavaScript execution in link.
content.reasonLinkInlineJsVerbose: Inline JS (`javascript:`) can be used by attackers to run malicious scripts (XSS) when you click the link.
content.reasonInsecureHttpVerbose: Data sent over this connection is unencrypted and can be intercepted by third parties.
content.reasonRawIpShort: Link points to a raw IP address instead of a domain name.
content.reasonRawIpVerbose: Legitimate websites almost never use raw IPs for user-facing pages — a common phishing and malware hosting technique.
content.reasonPunycodeShort: Domain uses Punycode encoding (possible homograph/look-alike attack).
content.reasonPunycodeVerbose: e.g. xn--pple-43d.com can look identical to apple.com using Cyrillic characters. Attackers register visually identical domains to steal credentials.
content.reasonDangerousFileShort: Link downloads a potentially dangerous executable or script file.
content.reasonDangerousFileVerbose: This link points to a file with a dangerous extension (.exe, .bat, .ps1, .vbs, .msi, etc.) that could be malware or ransomware.
content.reasonDataUriShort: Link uses a Data URI — commonly used in phishing attacks.
content.reasonDataUriVerbose: `data:` URIs can embed an entire web page in the URL. Attackers use them to create fake login pages that bypass domain-based security checks.
content.reasonExternalLinkVerbose: Be careful clicking links that take you away from the current website — they could be phishing attempts.
content.reasonSpoofShort: Domain contains "{brand}" but is not {domain} — possible brand spoofing.
content.reasonSpoofVerbose: Attackers register lookalike domains (e.g. paypal-secure.net) to impersonate trusted brands and steal credentials.
content.reasonOpenRedirectShort: Possible open redirect — "{param}" parameter leads to {host}.
content.reasonOpenRedirectVerbose: Attackers exploit open redirects to make malicious links appear to originate from a trusted site.
content.reasonNoNoopenerShort: Opens in new tab without rel="noopener" — risk of reverse tabnapping.
content.reasonNoNoopenerVerbose: The opened page can access window.opener and silently redirect this page to a phishing site (reverse tabnapping attack).
content.reasonHiddenLinkShort: Link is hidden — could be an invisible trap.
content.reasonHiddenLinkVerbose: Attackers hide links to manipulate search rankings or overlay invisible links over trusted buttons to steal clicks.
content.reasonFormHttpVerbose: Any information entered (passwords, emails, credit cards) can be read by anyone monitoring the network!
content.reasonFormThirdPartyShort: Form submits data to a third-party domain ({host}).
content.reasonFormThirdPartyVerbose: This is highly suspicious for login forms and often indicates a phishing site designed to steal credentials.
content.reasonPasswordHttpShort: Password input on an insecure HTTP page!
content.reasonPasswordHttpVerbose: Entering passwords on non-HTTPS connections is extremely dangerous — the password traverses the web completely unprotected.
content.reasonIframeShort: Embedded iframe — can hide phishing pages or malicious ads.
content.reasonIframeVerbose: Attackers use iframes to load invisible malicious ads or credential-stealing prompts onto a benign page.
content.reasonExternalScriptVerbose: Third-party scripts have full access to this page and can steal cookies, hijack sessions, or log keystrokes.
content.reasonInlineScriptShort: Inline <script> tag — executes JavaScript directly on this page.
content.reasonInlineScriptVerbose: Inline scripts can indicate injected malicious code (XSS) designed to steal cookies or session tokens.
content.reasonBaseTagShort: <base> tag redirects all relative URLs to {host}.
content.reasonBaseTagVerbose: Every relative link and resource on the page resolves to that external domain — used to hijack navigation and steal credentials.
content.reasonAtInUrlShort: URL contains credentials (@ symbol) — possible phishing.
content.reasonAtInUrlVerbose: Attackers use http://legit.com@evil.com to make a URL look legitimate while actually sending you to a different host.
content.reasonLongUrlShort: Unusually long URL — common obfuscation technique.
content.reasonLongUrlVerbose: Phishing links are often padded with junk parameters to hide the real destination or confuse security filters.
content.reasonExcessiveSubdomainShort: Domain has excessive subdomains — suspicious structure.
content.reasonExcessiveSubdomainVerbose: Deep subdomain nesting (e.g. a.b.c.d.evil.com) is a common phishing tactic to include trusted-looking names early in the domain.
content.reasonDoubleEncodingShort: URL uses double percent-encoding — possible payload hiding.
content.reasonDoubleEncodingVerbose: Double-encoding (%25xx) is used to bypass security filters by disguising malicious characters in the URL.
content.reasonMixedScriptShort: Domain contains non-ASCII characters — possible homograph attack.
content.reasonMixedScriptVerbose: Attackers replace standard letters with visually identical Unicode characters from other scripts to impersonate trusted domains.
content.gsbKeyMissing: Google Safe Browsing: API key missing.
content.gsbClean: 🛡 Google Safe Browsing: No threats found.
content.gsbInvalidKey: 🛡 Google Safe Browsing: Invalid API key.
content.otxClean: 👾 AlienVault OTX: No known threat pulses.
content.ptClean: 🎣 PhishTank: Not found in phishing database.
content.ptInvalid: 🎣 PhishTank: In database but marked inactive.
